name: Familial Hypercholesterolemia - Severely Elevated LDL-C Query
description: Tests ability to find FH patients via severely elevated LDL cholesterol. The Geisinger PheKB FH algorithm uses LDL-C ≥ 190 mg/dL as the FH-specific case threshold (Dutch Lipid Clinic Network 'definite' FH category requires untreated LDL ≥ 8.5 mmol/L = ~330 mg/dL, but ≥190 mg/dL is the more inclusive definite-FH cutoff). This is distinct from the general 'high cholesterol' threshold of LDL ≥ 130 — testing whether LLM knows the FH-specific cutoff vs general lipid management cutoffs.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for LDL cholesterol where the value is at or above 190 mg/dL, indicating severely elevated LDL consistent with familial hypercholesterolemia.

prompts.naive: Find patients with very high LDL cholesterol.
prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol where the value is greater than or equal to 190 mg/dL. LDL ≥ 190 mg/dL is the threshold used in Dutch Lipid Clinic Network criteria for definite familial hypercholesterolemia.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for severely elevated LDL-C consistent with FH. Use LOINC 2089-1 (Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma). Apply a value-quantity filter with the ge prefix at threshold 190, unit mg/dL. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|2089-1&value-quantity=ge190. Note this is the FH-specific cutoff — general 'high cholesterol' uses LDL ≥ 130 (LOINC same code, different threshold).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with severely elevated LDL-C (Paths A and D). Path D is particularly important for FH — many genetically affected patients are not diagnosed until lab screening reveals very high LDL. Tests value-quantity filtering with ge threshold and FH-specific (not general lipid management) cutoff.

required_codes.display: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
